title: Main Control Board Error
shortDescription: The main electronic control board has detected an internal fault preventing dryer operation.
fullDescription: Error code F1E1 indicates a failure in the dryer's main electronic control board. The board has detected an internal memory, processing, or communication error that prevents it from operating the dryer correctly. This can be caused by power surges, component degradation, or manufacturing defects. A simple power reset may resolve temporary glitches, but persistent errors typically require board replacement.

causes.cause: Power surge or voltage spike damaged the control board
Control board component failure from age or heat exposure
Loose wiring harness connection to control board

symptoms: F1 E1 error displayed on control panel
Dryer does not start or respond to controls
Dryer stops mid-cycle and displays error
Control panel buttons or display behave erratically

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer or turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. This resets the control board and may clear a temporary software glitch. Restore power and test.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Electric dryers require 240V. Use a multimeter to verify the outlet provides proper voltage on both legs. Gas dryers use 120V. Voltage irregularities can cause board errors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and remove the top or rear panel to access the control board. Check all wiring harness connections for looseness, corrosion, or burn marks. Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace main control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after reset and wiring checks, the main control board must be replaced. Note the part number on the board, order the replacement, and swap the boards.

whenToCallPro: If a power cycle does not clear the error and wiring connections are solid, professional board replacement is recommended. Incorrect installation can cause further damage.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F1E1 control board error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F1E1?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges are a leading cause of control board failure. Using a dedicated surge protector for your dryer is highly recommended.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the control board?
faq.a: If the dryer is less than 8-10 years old, replacing the board is usually more cost-effective than purchasing a new dryer.

faq.q: Can I use a used control board?
faq.a: Used boards can work but carry risk. They may have hidden damage or be near the end of their lifespan. New or factory-refurbished boards are preferred.

title: Stuck Button on Control Panel
shortDescription: A button on the user interface panel is stuck or being continuously activated.
fullDescription: Error code F2E1 indicates that the main control board has detected a button on the dryer's user interface panel that is stuck in the pressed position. This can be caused by physical debris, a liquid spill, a defective touchpad, or a faulty user interface board. The dryer will not respond to other commands while this condition exists.

causes.cause: Button physically stuck due to residue or debris

symptoms: F2 E1 error displayed on control panel
Dryer beeps continuously or intermittently
A specific button feels stuck or unresponsive
Dryer starts or changes settings on its own

fixSteps.title: Clean the control panel
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe down the control panel with a slightly damp cloth. Clean around and between all buttons to remove sticky residue, lint, or spilled fabric softener.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. While unplugged, press each button multiple times to free any that may be stuck. Plug back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Inspect touchpad membrane
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the control panel housing. Check the touchpad membrane for moisture damage, deformation, or wear. Allow to dry completely if moisture is found.

fixSteps.title: Replace user interface board
fixSteps.instruction: If the touchpad or UI board is defective, replace the user interface assembly. Transfer any display overlay to the new board if it is a separate component.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and resetting do not resolve the issue and the touchpad or UI board needs replacement, a technician can help if you are uncomfortable with panel disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F2E1 stuck button error

faq.q: Can dryer sheet residue cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. Dryer sheet residue or fabric softener buildup on the control panel can cause buttons to become sticky and trigger this error.

faq.q: Is F2E1 a dangerous error?
faq.a: It is not dangerous but prevents normal operation. If the dryer starts on its own due to a stuck button, unplug it immediately.

faq.q: Can I use the dryer with this error?
faq.a: The dryer will likely not operate correctly. The stuck button may prevent other commands from being recognized. Resolve the issue first.

title: User Interface Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board has lost communication with the user interface board.
fullDescription: Error code F2E5 indicates a communication failure between the dryer's main control board and the user interface (UI) board. These boards exchange data via a serial communication line. This error means the connection has been lost or data is corrupted, usually due to a loose ribbon cable or a faulty board.

causes.cause: Loose or disconnected ribbon cable between boards

symptoms: F2 E5 error displayed
Control panel does not respond to any button presses
Display goes blank or shows garbled text
Dryer powers on but no user control is possible

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes to reset all electronics. Plug back in and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the control panel housing screws. Locate the ribbon cable connecting the UI board to the main board. Disconnect and firmly reseat at both ends.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for corrosion
fixSteps.instruction: Check both cable connectors for corroded or bent pins. Clean contacts with electrical contact cleaner if corrosion is found.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is intact, try replacing the UI board first (less expensive). If the error persists with a new UI board, the main board may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connections does not work and you are unsure which board is faulty, a technician can use diagnostic tools to pinpoint the failed component.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F2E5 communication error fix

faq.q: How do I know which board is bad?
faq.a: Start by replacing the ribbon cable (cheapest). Then try the UI board. If neither resolves the issue, the main control board is likely at fault.

faq.q: Can heat damage the ribbon cable?
faq.a: Yes. The dryer generates significant heat, and over time, the cable connections can degrade. Regular inspection during maintenance can catch this early.

faq.q: Is this a common dryer problem?
faq.a: Communication errors are moderately common, especially in dryers over 5 years old. Loose connections due to vibration are the most frequent cause.

title: Exhaust Thermistor Open/Short
shortDescription: The exhaust temperature sensor (thermistor) has an open or short circuit condition.
fullDescription: Error code F3E1 indicates that the exhaust thermistor, which monitors the temperature of air leaving the dryer drum, has failed electrically. The sensor circuit is either open (broken connection) or shorted. Without accurate exhaust temperature data, the dryer cannot properly regulate heat and may overheat or underheat.

causes.cause: Wiring harness damage from heat exposure

symptoms: F3 E1 error code displayed
Dryer does not heat or overheats
Dryer shuts off prematurely
Clothes take much longer to dry

fixSteps.title: Locate the exhaust thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The exhaust thermistor is typically located in the exhaust duct or on the blower housing near the lint filter area. It is a small sensor with two wires.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connection
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the thermistor connector for looseness, corrosion, or damage. Disconnect and reseat the connector. Ensure the wires are not melted or frayed from heat.

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure its resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature, it should read approximately 10,000-12,000 ohms (10K-12K). An open circuit (infinite) or near-zero reading indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: If the thermistor reads out of range, replace it. Remove the mounting screw or clip, disconnect the wiring, install the new thermistor, and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor and wiring test correctly but the error persists, the control board may have a faulty input circuit requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F3E1 thermistor error fix

faq.q: What does the exhaust thermistor do?
faq.a: It measures the temperature of air leaving the dryer drum. This helps the control board regulate heat to prevent overheating and ensure proper drying.

faq.q: Can I run the dryer without the thermistor?
faq.a: No. Running the dryer without a functioning thermistor removes an important safety feature that prevents overheating and potential fire hazards.

faq.q: Are dryer thermistors expensive?
faq.a: No. Thermistors are very affordable, typically $8-25. The main cost of professional repair is labor.

title: Inlet Thermistor Open/Short
shortDescription: The inlet temperature sensor has an open or short circuit, preventing proper temperature monitoring.
fullDescription: Error code F3E2 indicates that the inlet thermistor (the sensor monitoring incoming air temperature to the drum) has an electrical fault. This sensor helps the control board determine how much heat to add based on the incoming air temperature. A failed sensor causes inaccurate heating control.

causes.cause: Disconnected or damaged wiring to sensor
Heat damage to thermistor from restricted airflow

symptoms: F3 E2 error code displayed
Dryer runs but does not heat properly
Clothes come out damp or excessively hot
Dryer shuts off too soon or runs too long

fixSteps.title: Locate the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The inlet thermistor is typically located near the heater assembly or on the air inlet duct. Consult the wiring diagram for your model.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring and connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring for heat damage, melting, or disconnection. The inlet area can get very hot, especially if airflow has been restricted.

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and test with a multimeter. At room temperature, expect approximately 10,000-12,000 ohms. Replace if the reading is significantly off, open, or shorted.

fixSteps.title: Replace thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty thermistor and install the replacement. Ensure it is securely mounted and the connector is firmly attached.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor tests within spec but the error persists, the control board input circuit may be faulty. A technician can test and replace the board.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F3E2 inlet thermistor error

faq.q: What is the difference between the inlet and exhaust thermistor?
faq.a: The inlet thermistor measures air temperature entering the drum, while the exhaust thermistor measures air temperature leaving. Both are needed for proper heat regulation.

faq.q: Can a clogged lint trap cause thermistor failure?
faq.a: Yes. Restricted airflow causes the dryer to run hotter than normal, which can damage temperature sensors over time. Always keep the lint trap clean.

faq.q: Are the inlet and exhaust thermistors the same part?
faq.a: Sometimes yes. Many models use the same thermistor part number for both locations. Check your model's parts list to confirm.

title: Moisture Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The moisture sensor bars are not providing a valid signal to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code F3E3 indicates that the moisture sensor is not functioning correctly. The moisture sensor consists of two metal bars inside the drum that detect the moisture level of the clothes by measuring electrical conductivity between them. A failed sensor causes the dryer to run too long, too short, or not sense when clothes are dry.

causes.cause: Moisture sensor bars coated with dryer sheet residue
Moisture sensor bars corroded or damaged
Control board moisture sensing circuit failure

symptoms: F3 E3 error code displayed
Dryer runs for too long or shuts off too quickly
Auto-dry cycles do not work correctly
Clothes come out still damp or over-dried

fixSteps.title: Clean moisture sensor bars
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the two metal sensor bars inside the drum (usually near the lint trap). Clean them with rubbing alcohol and a soft cloth to remove dryer sheet residue, fabric softener, or mineral deposits.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and check the wiring connections from the moisture sensor to the control board. Ensure the connectors are secure and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: With the sensor disconnected, test between the two sensor leads. The resistance should change when you touch the bars with a wet cloth vs. dry. If no change, the sensor or wiring is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace moisture sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not restore function, replace the moisture sensor assembly. Remove the mounting screws, disconnect the wiring, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the bars and replacing the sensor does not fix the issue, the control board may have a faulty sensing circuit requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F3E3 moisture sensor error fix

faq.q: Why does dryer sheet residue affect the moisture sensor?
faq.a: Dryer sheets leave a waxy film on the sensor bars that insulates them, preventing accurate moisture detection. Clean monthly if you use dryer sheets.

faq.q: Can I use timed dry instead?
faq.a: Yes. If the moisture sensor is not working, timed dry cycles will still work since they do not rely on the sensor. However, fix the sensor for optimal performance.

faq.q: Does fabric softener also affect the sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Liquid fabric softener can also leave residue. Using dryer balls instead of fabric softener or dryer sheets can help keep sensors clean.

title: Restricted Airflow Detected
shortDescription: The dryer has detected restricted exhaust airflow, causing potential overheating.
fullDescription: Error code F4E1 indicates that the dryer's airflow sensor system has detected restricted air movement through the exhaust vent system. This is a critical safety alert because restricted airflow is the leading cause of dryer fires. The exhaust vent may be clogged with lint, crushed, kinked, or too long, preventing proper air circulation needed to carry heat and moisture away from the drum.

causes.cause: Lint buildup in exhaust vent duct
Crushed or kinked exhaust vent hose
Exhaust vent too long or has too many turns
Blocked exterior vent hood or damper

symptoms: F4 E1 error code displayed
Dryer takes much longer to dry clothes
Dryer exterior is very hot to the touch
Excessive lint around the dryer or on clothing
Humid or hot laundry room when dryer is running

fixSteps.title: Clean lint screen
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the lint screen thoroughly. Also clean the lint screen housing by vacuuming inside the slot where the screen sits.

fixSteps.title: Inspect exhaust vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dryer away from the wall. Check the exhaust vent hose for kinks, crushes, or disconnections. Replace foil or plastic vent hoses with rigid or semi-rigid metal duct.

fixSteps.title: Clean exhaust vent duct
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the vent from the dryer and clean the entire duct run using a dryer vent cleaning brush kit. Clean from both the dryer end and the exterior vent hood.

fixSteps.title: Check exterior vent hood
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and locate the dryer vent hood on the exterior wall. Verify the damper opens freely and is not blocked by lint, bird nests, or ice. Clean as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the exhaust vent runs through walls or the roof and you cannot clean it yourself, professional dryer vent cleaning services can thoroughly clean and inspect the entire duct system.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F4E1 restricted airflow fix

faq.q: Is restricted airflow really a fire hazard?
faq.a: Yes. Lint is highly flammable. According to the U.S. Fire Administration, clogged dryer vents are responsible for thousands of home fires annually. This is a serious safety issue.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dryer vent?
faq.a: Clean the exhaust vent at least once a year, or more frequently if you do many loads per week. The lint screen should be cleaned before every load.

faq.q: Can I use a vinyl or foil vent hose?
faq.a: No. Vinyl and thin foil vent hoses are fire hazards. Use only rigid or semi-rigid metal duct, which is required by most building codes.

title: Exhaust High Temperature Limit
shortDescription: The dryer exhaust temperature has exceeded the safe maximum operating temperature.
fullDescription: Error code F4E3 indicates that the exhaust air temperature has exceeded the safety threshold. The dryer has shut down heating to prevent fire or damage. This is almost always caused by restricted airflow that traps heat in the dryer, but can also indicate a failed gas valve (gas dryers), cycling thermostat, or heating element issue.

causes.cause: Heating element shorted to ground (electric dryers)
Gas valve not cycling properly (gas dryers)

symptoms: F4 E3 error code displayed
Dryer shuts off mid-cycle due to overheating
Dryer exterior extremely hot
Burning smell from dryer
High limit thermostat trips repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Stop using dryer immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Do not run the dryer until the cause is identified. Overheating is a fire hazard. Allow the dryer to cool completely before inspection.

fixSteps.title: Clean entire exhaust system
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly clean the lint screen, lint screen housing, exhaust vent hose, and exterior vent hood. Use a vent cleaning brush to reach deep into the ductwork.

fixSteps.title: Test cycling thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Locate the cycling thermostat (on the exhaust duct). Test for continuity at room temperature - it should be closed (0 ohms). Replace if open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect heating element (electric) or gas valve (gas)
fixSteps.instruction: For electric dryers, check the heating element for shorts to ground. For gas dryers, observe the gas valve operation during a cycle. Replace faulty components.

whenToCallPro: Any overheating issue should be taken seriously. If cleaning the vent does not resolve the problem, a technician should inspect the heating system components.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F4E3 high temperature error fix

faq.q: Can this error cause a fire?
faq.a: The error itself is a safety feature that shuts down heating. However, the underlying cause (restricted airflow or faulty heating component) is a genuine fire risk that must be addressed.

faq.q: My dryer has a burning smell. Is this related?
faq.a: A burning smell combined with F4E3 is very concerning. It may indicate lint burning inside the dryer or vent. Stop using the dryer and clean the entire system immediately.

faq.q: Will the dryer work after it cools down?
faq.a: It may start again after cooling, but do not run it until you have identified and fixed the cause of overheating. Running the dryer with restricted airflow is dangerous.

title: Exhaust Temperature Not Rising
shortDescription: The dryer exhaust temperature is not increasing as expected, indicating a heating system failure.
fullDescription: Error code F4E4 indicates that the control board expected the exhaust temperature to rise during a heated cycle, but the thermistor readings show little or no temperature increase. This means the dryer's heating system is not working. On electric dryers, this is typically a heating element or relay failure. On gas dryers, it is usually an igniter, gas valve, or flame sensor issue.

causes.cause: Heating relay on control board failed

symptoms: F4 E4 error displayed
Dryer runs but produces no heat
Clothes come out cold and damp
Dryer drum turns normally but no warmth is felt

fixSteps.title: Check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Locate the thermal fuse (usually on the exhaust duct or blower housing). Test for continuity with a multimeter. A blown fuse reads open (no continuity). Replace if blown, and also identify why it blew.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element (electric)
fixSteps.instruction: For electric dryers, test the heating element for continuity. Also check for shorts to ground by testing between each terminal and the element housing. Replace if open or shorted.

fixSteps.title: Test gas igniter (gas)
fixSteps.instruction: For gas dryers, observe the igniter during a cycle. It should glow bright orange and ignite the gas within 90 seconds. If it glows but gas does not ignite, the gas valve coils are likely bad.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Based on testing, replace the heating element, thermal fuse, gas igniter, or gas valve coils. After replacement, test the dryer to confirm proper heating.

whenToCallPro: If you have a gas dryer and are not comfortable working with gas appliances, a technician should handle gas valve and igniter repairs.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F4E4 no heat error fix

faq.q: Why did my thermal fuse blow?
faq.a: Thermal fuses blow due to overheating, usually from restricted airflow. If you replace the fuse without fixing the airflow issue, it will blow again.

faq.q: Can I test the heating element without removing it?
faq.a: You can test continuity with the wires disconnected while the element is still installed. However, checking for ground shorts requires access to the element housing.

faq.q: Is it safe to work on a gas dryer?
faq.a: Gas dryer repairs involving the gas valve should be done carefully. If you smell gas at any point, turn off the gas supply and contact a professional immediately.

title: Communication Error - Serial Interface
shortDescription: Communication between the dryer's electronic boards has been lost.
fullDescription: Error code F6E1 indicates a communication failure on the dryer's internal serial data bus. The main control board and user interface board communicate via a serial line, and this error means data transmission has been interrupted. Loose connections are the most common cause.
symptoms: F6 E1 error code displayed
Dryer stops mid-cycle
Control panel unresponsive
Display may go blank or show error

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes, then plug back in. This may restore communication.

fixSteps.title: Reseat all connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Access the control area and reseat all wiring harness connectors between the main board and the UI board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check all communication wiring for heat damage, chafing, or pinch points. The dryer's heat can degrade wiring over time.

fixSteps.title: Replace boards if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If connections are sound, try replacing the UI board first, then the main board if the error persists.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connections does not fix the issue, a technician can determine which board is faulty before purchasing replacements.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F6E1 communication error fix

faq.q: Is F6E1 always a board failure?
faq.a: No. Most F6E1 errors are resolved by reseating loose connections. Always check wiring before assuming a board is bad.

faq.q: Can dryer vibration cause this?
faq.a: Yes. Vibration from normal operation can gradually loosen connectors over time.

faq.q: Why does this happen intermittently?
faq.a: Intermittent communication errors often indicate a loose but not completely disconnected connector. The connection may work when cool but lose contact when heat causes expansion.

title: Communication Error - Invalid Data
shortDescription: The control boards are exchanging corrupted data signals.
fullDescription: Error code F6E2 indicates that communication between control boards is occurring but the data is corrupted or unreadable. This differs from F6E1 (complete loss) in that the connection exists but data integrity is compromised.

causes.cause: Failing board component affecting data processing

symptoms: F6 E2 error code on display
Erratic dryer behavior
Unexpected cycle changes
Intermittent errors that clear on restart

fixSteps.title: Power cycle and test
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. Plug into a surge protector. Run diagnostic mode to check for errors.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all wiring harnesses for heat damage, chafing, or deterioration. Replace any damaged wiring.

fixSteps.title: Verify power quality
fixSteps.instruction: Check the dryer outlet for proper voltage. Electric dryers need stable 240V. Voltage fluctuations can corrupt data signals.

fixSteps.title: Replace boards
fixSteps.instruction: If wiring is intact and power is clean, replace the UI board, then the main board if needed.

whenToCallPro: Data corruption issues are difficult to diagnose without specialized equipment. Professional help is recommended if basic checks do not resolve the issue.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F6E2 data error fix

faq.q: What causes data corruption between boards?
faq.a: Damaged wiring, electromagnetic interference from other appliances, power fluctuations, or deteriorating circuit board components can all corrupt serial data.

faq.q: Should I replace both boards?
faq.a: No. Diagnose which board is faulty first. Replacing both is wasteful if only one is bad.

faq.q: Can I prevent this from happening?
faq.a: Using a quality surge protector, ensuring stable power supply, and keeping the dryer in a well-ventilated area to reduce heat stress on electronics can all help.

title: Communication Error - No UI Board Detected
shortDescription: The main control board cannot detect the user interface board during startup.
fullDescription: Error code F6E3 indicates that during the initial power-on handshake, the main control board did not receive a response from the user interface board. This typically means the UI board is disconnected, the ribbon cable is loose, or the UI board has failed completely.

causes.cause: Disconnected ribbon cable to UI board

symptoms: F6 E3 may appear on diagnostic LED only
Display completely blank
No buttons respond
Dryer powers on but is completely unresponsive

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove control panel screws. Locate and firmly reseat the ribbon cable at both the UI board and main board ends.

fixSteps.title: Check cable for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the ribbon cable for cuts, crimps, or heat damage along its entire length. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test UI board power
fixSteps.instruction: With caution, verify that power is reaching the UI board connector. If no power, trace the supply.

fixSteps.title: Replace UI board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is good but the UI board is not responding, replace the user interface board.

whenToCallPro: If a new UI board and cable do not fix the issue, the main control board may have a faulty port.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer F6E3 no display error fix

faq.q: Why is my dryer display completely blank?
faq.a: F6E3 means the main board cannot find the UI board. The most common cause is a loose ribbon cable.

faq.q: Can heat damage the ribbon cable?
faq.a: Yes. Unlike washers, dryers generate significant heat that can affect wiring and connections over time.

faq.q: Is the UI board the same as the control board?
faq.a: No. The UI board handles buttons and display. The main control board runs the dryer's heating, motor, and cycle logic.

title: Power Failure
shortDescription: The dryer has experienced a power interruption during a drying cycle.
fullDescription: The PF (Power Failure) code indicates that the dryer lost power during operation and has since regained power. This is an informational code, not a malfunction. It tells the user that a power interruption occurred and the cycle was stopped. The user needs to restart or resume the cycle.

causes.cause: Intermittent wiring issue in home electrical

symptoms: PF displayed on control panel
Dryer is stopped and not running
Clothes inside are still damp
Time remaining display may show incomplete cycle

fixSteps.title: Press Start to resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start button to resume the drying cycle from where it was interrupted. The dryer should continue the remaining time.

fixSteps.title: Select new cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If you prefer, cancel the interrupted cycle and select a new one. Press Start to begin.

fixSteps.title: Check circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: If PF appears frequently, check the dryer's circuit breaker. Electric dryers use a double-pole 30-amp breaker. Ensure it is not tripping. A tripping breaker indicates an electrical issue.

fixSteps.title: Inspect power connections
fixSteps.instruction: Check the dryer power cord for a secure connection at the outlet. For hardwired dryers, verify the terminal block connections are tight.

whenToCallPro: If the PF code appears repeatedly without any known power outage, an electrician should check the dryer circuit for loose connections or an undersized breaker.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer PF power failure code

faq.q: Is PF an error code?
faq.a: No. PF is an informational code indicating the dryer lost and then regained power during a cycle. Simply restart or resume the cycle.

faq.q: Why does my dryer keep showing PF?
faq.a: Frequent PF codes without power outages suggest a loose power connection, a failing breaker, or a wiring problem in the dryer circuit.

faq.q: Will the dryer restart automatically after a power outage?
faq.a: No. For safety, the dryer does not restart automatically. You must press Start to resume or begin a new cycle.

title: Low or No Line Voltage (240V)
shortDescription: The electric dryer is not receiving proper 240V power supply.
fullDescription: The L2 error code appears on electric Maytag dryers when one leg of the 240V power supply is missing or below the acceptable threshold. Electric dryers require two 120V legs to form 240V. If one leg is lost (due to a tripped breaker, loose connection, or outlet problem), the dryer may power on (120V controls) but will not heat (heating element requires 240V).

causes.cause: One leg of 240V circuit breaker tripped
Loose power cord connection at outlet or terminal block

symptoms: L2 error code displayed
Dryer drum turns but no heat is produced
Display and controls work but dryer does not heat
Dryer may show PF error simultaneously

fixSteps.title: Check circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the electrical panel and check the dryer's double-pole breaker. Both halves must be in the ON position. If one has tripped, turn both OFF then back ON. If it trips again, call an electrician.

fixSteps.title: Test outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test the dryer outlet. You should read approximately 240V between the two hot terminals, and 120V between each hot terminal and neutral/ground.

fixSteps.title: Check power cord connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and inspect the power cord prongs for damage, burn marks, or corrosion. At the dryer terminal block, verify all connections are tight.

fixSteps.title: Replace power cord if damaged
fixSteps.instruction: If the power cord shows damage or a broken internal wire, replace it with a matching 3-prong or 4-prong cord rated for 30 amps.

whenToCallPro: If the breaker repeatedly trips or the outlet voltage is incorrect, an electrician should inspect the dryer circuit. Do not continue using the dryer with improper voltage.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer L2 no heat low voltage fix

faq.q: Why does my dryer turn on but not heat?
faq.a: The controls run on 120V (one leg of the circuit) while the heating element needs 240V (both legs). If one leg is lost, the dryer appears to work but cannot heat.

faq.q: Does this apply to gas dryers?
faq.a: No. Gas dryers use 120V and do not have the L2 error. This code is specific to electric dryers requiring 240V.

faq.q: Can a loose prong in the outlet cause L2?
faq.a: Yes. Worn outlets may not grip the power cord prongs tightly enough, causing intermittent contact. Have the outlet replaced if prongs feel loose.

title: Restricted Airflow Warning
shortDescription: The dryer has detected limited exhaust airflow and is alerting the user to check the vent system.
fullDescription: The AF (Airflow) code is a warning indicator that the dryer has detected reduced exhaust airflow. This is similar to F4E1 but is a less severe warning that appears before the dryer reaches a critical airflow restriction. It serves as an early alert to clean the vent system before the problem becomes severe enough to cause overheating or a fire hazard.

causes.cause: Gradual lint accumulation in exhaust vent

symptoms: AF displayed during or after a cycle
Slightly longer drying times than normal
More lint than usual on clothing
Laundry room feels warmer when dryer is running

fixSteps.title: Clean lint screen
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the lint screen. Wash with warm soapy water if coated with fabric softener residue. Allow to dry completely before reinserting.

fixSteps.title: Clean lint trap housing
fixSteps.instruction: Use a long narrow brush or vacuum attachment to clean inside the lint screen housing. Lint accumulates in areas the screen cannot catch.

fixSteps.title: Clean exhaust vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the exhaust vent from the dryer and clean the entire duct run with a vent cleaning brush. Also clean the exterior vent hood.

fixSteps.title: Check vent configuration
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the vent run is not excessively long (under 25 feet of straight duct) and does not have more than two 90-degree elbows. Replace any flexible foil or vinyl duct with rigid metal.

whenToCallPro: Professional vent cleaning services are recommended if the vent runs through walls, floors, or the roof and cannot be easily accessed for DIY cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Maytag dryer AF airflow warning fix

faq.q: Can I continue to use the dryer with the AF code?
faq.a: Yes, temporarily. The AF code is an early warning. The dryer will still operate but you should clean the vent system promptly to prevent escalation to overheating.

faq.q: How do I reset the AF code?
faq.a: The AF code will clear automatically once the airflow issue is resolved. After cleaning, run a cycle and the code should not reappear.

faq.q: How long can a dryer vent be?
faq.a: Maximum recommended length is 25 feet of straight rigid duct. Subtract 5 feet for each 90-degree elbow and 2.5 feet for each 45-degree elbow.
